sourceMovie.title: The Ages of Lulu
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Ages of Lulu (1990) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Bigas Luna's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Raging Bull
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ages of Lulu clicked for you, Raging Bull (1980) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: My Fault
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ages of Lulu clicked for you, My Fault (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Ages of Lulu?
faq.answer: Try Raging Bull, My Fault, Shame—each shares DNA with The Ages of Lulu while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Ages of Lulu?
faq.answer: The Ages of Lulu (1990) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Ages of Lulu worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
